text: Scrap any unit with an unsalvageable subsystem fault.
details: Programming: day 1 code is simple. If the program is repairable, the correct output will run cleanly.
Gears: day 1 scrap is obvious. A cracked start or end drive means the unit is unsalvageable.
Wires: day 1 uses regular wiring only. If the first required lead is physically missing, scrap the unit.
Circuit Board: day 1 uses exact sum / grouped regions only. If a charge cell is isolated behind walls, scrap the unit.

text: Scrap any unit that fails subsystem compliance verification.
details: Programming: day 2 code may drift. Scrap outputs that become invalid, protocol-broken, or visibly corrupted.
Gears: day 2 adds rust and moving rejection markers. If a rejected gear is unavoidable, scrap the unit.
Wires: day 2 adds green and orange power filters. If any required route is forced red or reaches a module with the wrong energy, scrap it.
Circuit Board: day 2 adds < regions and = links. Scrap boards with a broken glyph marker or an isolated charge cell.

text: Scrap any unit showing hazardous instability, contamination, or unsafe discharge.
details: Programming: scrap units showing the green debugger bug with red sparks. That is hazardous corruption, not normal drift.
Gears: day 3 adds deadlock clamp dead space and red spark instability. If a sparking gear state exists, scrap the unit.
Wires: day 3 expands the board and adds multiple power sources. Neutral, orange, and green are valid; red crackling modules mean scrap.
Circuit Board: day 3 adds != links and mixed rule combinations. Scrap earlier fault states plus any unit carrying a corrupted red domino.

text: QUEST: The shady umbrella wants you to help start a rebellion against the machine line.
details: Unlocked through a private comms proposition from the Rebellious Umbrella.
This is a story lead, not a payroll directive. Keep an ear on the comms channel.
If the line starts talking about rebellion again, check the chat feed before filing your ruling.
